title: NOC 52121 – Interior Designers and Interior Decorators | GTR Immigration
description: As Canada’s real estate and commercial sectors continue to evolve, NOC 52121 (Interior designers and interior decorators) professionals are in high demand to create functional, aesthetic spaces. Whether it’s sustainable residential design or commercial ergonomic planning, Canada values the expertise of international designers. This TEER 2 occupation offers unique opportunities for self-employment and corporate careers alike.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level2Major Group52 – Technical occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group521 – Technical occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5212 – Design occupationsNOC Code52121

duties: Consult with clients to determine needs, preferences, and safety requirements.
Develop detailed plans and 3D models using CAD software.
Advise on the selection of colors, finishes, and materials.
Estimate costs and materials required for a project.
Coordinate with architects, engineers, and contractors to ensure design feasibility.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in interior design is usually required.
For decorators, a college certificate or diploma in interior decoration is typically required.
The NCIDQ exam may be required after 6 years of combined study and work experience.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate. Employment is expected to remain stable, with significant opportunities arising from the retirement of existing professionals.
labour_market_demand: The demand is heavily concentrated in Ontario, B.C., and Quebec, driven by new construction and large-scale renovations. There is a specific niche for designers with LEED certification and expertise in sustainable building practices, as Canada moves toward stricter environmental regulations.
High Self-Employment Rate: Over 45% of professionals in this NOC are self-employed.
Diverse Projects: Opportunities in residential, commercial, and institutional sectors.
Professional Recognition: Clear pathways to certification via NCIDQ.
pathways: Self-Employed Persons Program: For world-class designers with significant independent experience.
Express Entry (FSW/CEC): Competitive for those with high language proficiency.
OINP Human Capital Priorities: For designers with high CRS scores.
